title: CLAT 2026 — Law Entrance Exam
meta_description: CLAT 2026 Dec 7, 2025. 22 National Law Universities. 150 passage-based MCQs. BA LLB & LLM. Top NLU: NLSIU, NALSAR, NUJS.

hero.one_liner: Common Law Admission Test — gateway to 22 National Law Universities including NLSIU Bangalore, NALSAR Hyderabad, NUJS Kolkata, NLU Delhi

what_is_it: CLAT (Common Law Admission Test) is unified entrance exam for admission to 5-year integrated BA LLB and 1-year LLM programs at 22 National Law Universities (NLUs) across India. NLUs are India's premier law schools — equivalent to IITs for law. Graduates from top NLUs (NLSIU Bangalore, NALSAR Hyderabad, NUJS Kolkata, NLU Delhi, NLU Jodhpur, NALSAR, Symbiosis) get recruited at ₹15–30 LPA by top law firms, corporates, and international organizations. A legal career through NLU is one of the most lucrative professional paths after engineering and medicine in India.

CLAT is 100% passage-based comprehension exam — unlike other entrance tests with straight knowledge questions. All 150 MCQs come from passages (100–500 words). You read passage, answer 4–5 questions from it. Sections: English Language (28 Qs), Current Affairs & GK (35 Qs), Legal Reasoning (35 Qs), Logical Reasoning (28 Qs), Quantitative Techniques (14 Qs). No rote learning. Success requires: (1) Speed reading (500-word passage in 4 minutes), (2) Analytical thinking (not knowledge), (3) Current affairs awareness (23% of exam), (4) Basic legal reasoning (no prior law knowledge required — passages teach the law). Exam duration: 2 hours for 150 questions = 48 seconds per question.

For ambitious students interested in law, NLU education is transformative. You get: (1) World-class faculty, (2) Access to international law schools (student exchange programs), (3) Internships at top law firms (6-month internships common), (4) Mooting and debate culture, (5) Networks with future judges, lawyers, corporate leaders. The first-year itself shapes your legal thinking — NLUs teach jurisprudence, constitutional law, research methods that prepare you for specialized law practice or civil services (many NLU graduates clear UPSC). After NLU, your career options: top law firms (Luthra & Associates, J Sagar, Shardul Amarchand earn ₹25–50 LPA for senior associates), corporate legal positions, litigation chambers (judges' chambers, ₹10–50 LPA depending on seniority), public interest law, academia.

key_details.label: PG Eligibility
key_details.value: LLB/BA LLB degree (50% for Gen, 45% for SC/ST)

exam_pattern.description: 150 passage-based MCQs. Read passage, answer questions from it. Sections divided: English (28), CA/GK (35), Legal Reasoning (35), Logical Reasoning (28), Quantitative (14).

major_posts.pay_level: ₹15–30 LPA at top firms (Luthra, Shardul, JSagar)

extra_sections.heading: Top NLUs: Cutoff Ranks & Placements (2025 Data)
CLAT Success Formula & Study Plan
content:
6-Month Preparation Timeline (Starting June for Dec Exam):

Month 1–2 (June–July): Foundation building. Read 2–3 quality newspapers daily (The Hindu, Indian Express, BBC News).
Understand question types through sample CLAT passages. Practice reading 500-word passages in 5 minutes (building speed).
Join a coaching institute or online course (CLAT typically needs guided preparation). Start daily 2-hour prep sessions.
Month 3 (August): Intensive section-wise focus. English: daily 30-minute reading + comprehension practice.
Current Affairs: weekly summary of 20 major events. Legal Reasoning: understand basics (rule-application), practice 10 passages daily.
Logical Reasoning: syllogisms, analogies, sequencing (similar to CAT-style reasoning). Quantitative: basic arithmetic/percentage (only 14 Qs, don't overspend time).
Month 4 (September): Full-length mock tests. Start taking 2 full-length mocks per week.
Analyze: Which passage type takes longest? Where are accuracy lapses?
Speed issues? Refine strategy based on mock performance.
Month 5 (October): High-volume practice. Take 3–4 mocks per week.
Read 2–3 current affairs sources daily. Revise all concepts.
Identify shortcut patterns. Build test-taking stamina (2-hour focus).
Month 6 (November): Final preparation. Take 1 mock every 2 days.
Sleep well. Avoid learning new concepts this late; focus on refinement.
December exam day: calm, hydrated, confident.

preparation_tips: Read newspapers DAILY — The Hindu or Indian Express. Current Affairs is 23% of CLAT (35 questions). Track: government policy changes, Supreme Court judgments, international developments, constitutional amendments, economic news. Make a 'Current Affairs notebook' with monthly summaries.

Speed reading is non-negotiable — practice reading 400–500 word passages in <3 minutes while retaining 70% content. Start with slow reading (10 min per passage) and gradually increase speed over 4–5 months of practice.

Legal Reasoning doesn't require law knowledge — passages teach law. Strategy: (1) Identify the rule from passage. (2) Identify the facts/situation. (3) Apply rule to situation. (4) Answer question. Think like a lawyer — logical application of rules to facts, not memorized law knowledge.

Mock tests are critical — take 2–3 full-length CLAT mocks per week from month 4 onwards (starting Sept 2025 for Dec exam). Analyze: Which passage types take longest? Where do you make comprehension errors? Is your speed adequate? Use mock insights to refine strategy.

faqs.question: Commerce/Science student can take CLAT UG?
faqs.answer: Yes. CLAT UG is open to ANY stream — Arts, Commerce, Science, Engineering. 12th pass with 45% (40% for SC/ST) is sufficient. Many successful NLU students come from non-Arts backgrounds — science graduates bring analytical thinking, commerce students bring practical business law perspective.

faqs.question: What's difference between CLAT and LSAT India?
faqs.answer: CLAT: 22 NLUs (India's best law schools). LSAT India: Private law schools (Jindal, IFIM, Christ, etc.) + international LLM applications. For Indian law career, CLAT is primary. If aiming for top-tier Indian legal education, clear CLAT. LSAT India is backup or for international aspirations.

faqs.question: Is 5-year BA LLB after 12th worth it?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Starting law at 18, you're practicing lawyer by 23 — while engineering/medical peers are still studying. By 30, senior associates at law firms earn ₹40–80 LPA. Career trajectory is steep. Quality of life as lawyer is good — cerebral work, client interactions, courtroom drama, financial security. The main catch: law practice requires continuous learning; your first 2–3 years post-NLU involve intense work. But long-term, highly rewarding.

faqs.question: What rank is needed for NLSIU Bangalore?
faqs.answer: NLSIU (India's top law school, equivalent of IIT): Rank 1–70 (approx). Extremely competitive. You need to score 130+/150 (87%+) in CLAT to get this rank. For other top NLUs (NALSAR, NUJS, NLU Delhi): Rank 70–250 (achievable with 110–130 CLAT score = 73–87%).

syllabus.subject: English Language
syllabus.topics: Reading comprehension (passage-based), vocabulary in context, grammar, sentence correction, para jumbles, critical reasoning from passages

syllabus.subject: Current Affairs & GK
syllabus.topics: Current affairs of last 12 months, legal news, Supreme Court judgments, new laws & amendments, international affairs, awards, sports, economy

syllabus.subject: Legal Reasoning
syllabus.topics: Passage-based legal principles, applying legal rules to fact situations, constitutional law basics, criminal law, contract law, tort law, family law

syllabus.subject: Logical Reasoning
syllabus.topics: Passage-based arguments, identifying assumptions, strengthening/weakening arguments, drawing conclusions, analogical reasoning, cause & effect

syllabus.subject: Quantitative Techniques
syllabus.topics: Data interpretation, percentage, ratio, average, basic algebra, profit & loss, time & work, data sufficiency — all passage/data based (not calculation heavy)

study_plan.tasks: CLAT is 100% passage-based since 2020. Build reading speed — read The Hindu editorial daily. Legal reasoning basics — study Indian Constitution (Part III & IV), basic criminal law concepts, contract law principles. Current affairs notes daily.

Solve CLAT previous papers (2020-2025 new pattern). Practice 5 RC passages daily under timed conditions. Legal reasoning mock sets. Logical reasoning — argument-based questions. Quantitative — basic DI practice.

Full mocks weekly. Current affairs of last 12 months — focus on legal/constitutional news, Supreme Court landmark judgments, new laws passed. Target: 100+ out of 150 for top 5 NLUs. Time management: 2 hours for 150 questions.
